For \(x\) in \([0, 1)\), \(2x\) changes every \(0.5\), \(4x\) every \(0.25\), \(6x\) every \(0.1667\), and \(8x\) every \(0.125\). The least common multiple of these intervals is \(0.125\), so we only need to check \(x\) in intervals of \(0.125\). By evaluating the expression for \(x\) in these intervals, we find that the expression can take on every integer value from \(0\) to \(20\) as \(x\) ranges from \(0\) to \(1\).
